error.signature: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'keras' / ImportError: cannot import name 'layers' from 'keras'

verdict.summary: Keras import fails after TF 2.16+ migration. Keras was unbundled from TensorFlow in 2.16; standalone keras 3.x has a different API.

dead_ends.action: pip install keras separately alongside tensorflow
dead_ends.why_fails: TF 2.16+ uses keras 3.x which has breaking API changes. Installing it does not restore tf.keras behavior.
Fragile workaround that breaks with nested imports and keras submodules.

workarounds.action: Use tf.keras instead of standalone keras imports
workarounds.how: from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense  # NOT from keras.layers import Dense
pip install tf-keras; import os; os.environ['TF_USE_LEGACY_KERAS'] = '1'

workarounds.action: Pin TensorFlow < 2.16 if you cannot migrate yet
workarounds.how: pip install 'tensorflow>=2.15,<2.16'  # last version with bundled keras 2.x
